Want to shave seconds off your race time? It starts with your technique in the water.

One of the most overlooked fundamentals in competitive swimming is body rotation. A powerful, controlled hip-to-shoulder rotation doesn't just drive your stroke — it reduces drag, lengthens your reach, and transfers energy from your core to every pull. Elite swimmers rotate up to 45 degrees on each stroke cycle. If you're not training this deliberately, you're leaving speed on the table.

Try this drill in your next session: Single-arm freestyle with a kickboard. Focus entirely on initiating each pull with a hip drive rather than a shoulder pull. After 200m you'll feel the difference immediately — more power, less effort.

At Velocx NZ, our Italian-crafted performance suits are designed to move with your body, not against it. The compressive fit supports your core rotation while our hydrophobic fabrics cut through resistance so your technique does exactly what it's supposed to do — make you faster.
